channel.title: Statistics on Test
channel.description: Recent content in Statistics on Test

item.title: Probability_and_inferential_statistics
item.description: Introduction This is some of the topics in USF (University of San Francisco) MS Data Science prerequisite requirements of Probability and Inferential Statistics. Law of Total Probability Definition A fundamental rule relating marginal probabilities to conditional probabilities. In mathematical statement, it looks simple and intuitive: For any event A, $$ P(A) = \sum_n{P(A \cap B_n)} = \sum_n{P(A|B_n)P(B_n)}$$ , where ${B_n : n = 1, 2, 3, …}$ is a finite partition of a sample space.
